audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in rpc_client_stream.h are safe: (1) `message` in `on_received` is invariantly non-null — `_receiving_message` is always initialized via `new ReadBuffer()` in `reset_receiving_env()` before any message enters the queue; (2) `cntl` in `on_received` is explicitly null-checked at L170 before use; (3) `cntl` in send-path callbacks (`on_sending/on_sent/on_send_failed`) originates from the send queue, and `call_method` dereferences it before insertion, so a null can never reach the queue; (4) map values in destructor/`on_closed` are also non-null for the same reason.
